title: Letter to Peter Blackmore (Compaq EVP Sales & Services) — Post-9/11 HP-Compaq Merger Follow-up
author: Peter S. Kastner (Aberdeen Group, Chief Research Officer)
abstract: Short business letter from Peter Kastner to Peter Blackmore at Compaq, written in the immediate aftermath of the September 11, 2001 attacks and the HP-Compaq merger announcement. Follows up on a May 2001 Cambridge meeting on customer communications; transmits seven Aberdeen position papers on the HP-Compaq merger integration and proposes field-sales preparation and education program work for Aberdeen. Captures tone and relationship-management style of the Aberdeen era. Aberdeen-internal employer record.
importance_rationale: Brief but primary-source record of Aberdeen's active business-development approach to Compaq at a historically charged moment; valuable for employer-timeline context.
relevance_rationale: The letter-as-business-development artifact is a useful teaching example; topic-specific content (HP-Compaq merger) is largely of historical interest.
prescience_rationale: Document is business correspondence, not a predictive analysis.
